name: VEX Protocol
description: The trust layer for AI agents. Adversarial verification, temporal memory, and cryptographic proofs for production-grade AI.

VEX_PORT.description: The port the VEX server will bind to. Leave as 8080 or use Railway's provided PORT.

DATABASE_URL.description: The connection string to your PostgreSQL database. If omitted, VEX falls back to SQLite.

VEX_JWT_SECRET.description: Secure 32-character secret for JWT authentication. Generated automatically.

VEX_DEFAULT_PROVIDER.description: Select the primary LLM engine. Options: deepseek, openai, anthropic, mistral, ollama, or mock.

VEX_ADVERSARIAL.description: Enable Red/Blue adversarial debate for verified reasoning consensus.

OPENAI_API_KEY.description: OpenAI API Key (optional if using other providers)

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.description: Anthropic API Key (optional if using other providers)

DEEPSEEK_API_KEY.description: DeepSeek API Key (optional if using other providers)

MISTRAL_API_KEY.description: Mistral API Key (optional if using other providers)

VEX_HARDWARE_SEED.description: 64-character hex seed for hardware identity. If omitted in Railway, falls back to zero-seed with warning.

VEX_DEV_MODE.description: Set to 1 to bypass strict hardware requirements and use dev fallbacks.
